name: Green Thumb Industries Inc.
business: Green Thumb Industries Inc. manufactures and distributes a portfolio of cannabis consumer packaged goods brands (including &Shine, Beboe, Dogwalkers, Doctor Solomon's, Good Green, incredibles and RYTHM) to third-party retail stores and its own retail stores. The company also owns and operates retail cannabis stores under the RISE Dispensaries chain, selling both its own products and third-party products. As of December 31, 2025, Green Thumb has operations in fourteen U.S. markets.
sellsTo: Green Thumb owned retail stores (RISE Dispensaries)
patients and customers for medical and adult-use

exposures.sentence: Green Thumb's core business is manufacturing, distributing and marketing a portfolio of cannabis consumer packaged goods brands, including &Shine, Beboe, Dogwalkers, Doctor Solomon's, Good Green, incredibles and RYTHM (which we refer to as our Consumer Packaged Goods business).
Our branded products are produced in manufacturing facilities across thirteen U.S. states where we cultivate, process and manufacture cannabis consumer packaged goods.

The Company competes directly with other cannabis producers and retailers... More broadly, Green Thumb views manufacturers of other consumer products, such as consumable hemp, including consumable hemp-derived tetrahydrocannabinol (THC) variants such as Delta-8 THC and, Delta-9 THC (hemp-derived THC products) as direct competitors.

catalysts.event: Second closing of sale of intellectual property to RYTHM, Inc.
catalysts.sentence: in transactions that closed on May 20, 2025 and August 27, 2026, the Company, through sales of the equity interests of indirectly owned subsidiaries, sold the intellectual property related to those consumer packaged goods brands to RYTHM, Inc.

catalysts.event: Amendment to syndicated credit facility to borrow additional $50 million
catalysts.sentence: Subsequent to the quarter. on February 19, 2026, the Company amended its existing syndicated credit facility agreement, for the purpose of borrowing an additional $50,000 thousand (issued as Amended Credit Facility), as permitted under the original agreement.

catalysts.event: Effective date of hemp definition change in 2026 Appropriations Act
catalysts.sentence: Passed on November 12, 2025, the Continuing Appropriations and Extensions Act of 2026 (H.R. 5371) (the 2026 Appropriations Act ), includes a provision (section 781) to amend the definition of hemp in the 2018 Farm Bill to effectively prohibit the currently commercialized hemp-derived THC products, although the change does not become effective until November 12, 2026.

catalysts.event: Executive order directing rescheduling of cannabis to Schedule III
catalysts.sentence: On December 18, 2025, President Trump issued an Executive Order directing that cannabis be rescheduled from Schedule I to Schedule III.
